title: Missingness and selection-bias diagnostic

nature_skill.name: nature-figure
nature_skill.contract: quantitative grid; SVG/PDF/PNG; editable SVG/PDF text; source CSV traceability

core_conclusion: The approved top-five cohort has zero missing cells, while full-universe not-clean cells show a diagnostic selection boundary.
target_output: offline reader report and publication-editable static figure exports

panel_map.a: Observed and not-clean full-universe cells by stratum.
panel_map.b: Diagnostic score difference for not-clean cells where both means exist.
panel_map.d: Mapping-status classes contributing to the boundary.

evidence_hierarchy.hero_evidence: Observed and not-clean full-universe cells by stratum.
evidence_hierarchy.controls_or_boundary: Selection diagnostics cannot be promoted into criterion-validity evidence.

statistics_notes: Panel b shows descriptive mean-score differences only; no IPV-outcome association is computed.
image_integrity_notes: No raster scientific image manipulation; all charts are generated from corrected tabular sources.
reviewer_risk: Selection diagnostics cannot be promoted into criterion-validity evidence.
